name: Winnipeg (Canada)

colors.symbolism: the clear blue prairie sky over Winnipeg
the surrounding fields of wheat — the city's original primary economic activity

description: Per bend sinister: the upper-hoist half blue, the lower-fly half yellow, with the city's coat of arms charged in the centre at the divide.
history: Adopted by Winnipeg City Council on 1 October 1975 and registered as a trademark on 7 April 1976. The flag with the full coat of arms remains the official representation of the city for ceremonial and official purposes; a separate logo flag adopted in 2001 is used for promotional purposes.

features.symbolism: the blue upper hoist represents the clear prairie sky; the gold lower fly the surrounding wheat fields, the city's foundational economic resource

features.role: coat of arms of the City of Winnipeg
features.symbolism: the city's coat of arms, granted on the city's incorporation and used since 1975 as the central emblem of the flag
